title: Avatar and header upload via update_credentials
description:
## What
Add avatar and header image upload support to PATCH /api/v1/accounts/update_credentials. Accept multipart form-data with avatar and header file fields. Use magic byte MIME detection (not Content-Type header). Accept JPEG, PNG, WebP, GIF; convert HEIC to JPEG via the `image` crate. Enforce 2MB max for avatars. Generate thumbnail. Write atomically. Return updated Account with avatar/header URLs.

## Why
Profile images are the most visible part of any account. Without this, local personas appear as default grey silhouettes in all clients. Required for sub-epic 8-A completeness.

## Agent team pattern
- Spawn a Research subagent to: read activitypub-federation crate docs for actor document publicKey/image fields; read `image` crate docs for HEIC decode support and format detection; fetch mastodon.social live response for Account.avatar and Account.avatar_static fields including URL structure; document Mastodon's avatar size limits (2MB per their source).
- Spawn an Implementation subagent to: extend the update_credentials handler to accept multipart; detect MIME type from magic bytes (first 12 bytes: JPEG=FFD8FF, PNG=89504E47, WebP=52494646...57454250, GIF=47494638); reject unsupported types with 422; decode HEIC via `image` crate and re-encode as JPEG; resize avatar to 400x400 max using fast_image_resize; resize header to 1500x500 max; write file atomically (write to temp path, rename); insert media row with null post_id; update accounts.avatar_media_id or header_media_id; compute blurhash.
- Spawn a Test subagent to: write integration tests using real PNG/JPEG/WebP test fixtures (generated via `image` crate, not from the handler); verify MIME detection rejects a file with wrong bytes; verify 2MB limit is enforced; verify dimensions after resize; verify account avatar_url points to accessible path; verify atomic write (temp file gone after success).

## Test discipline
- Independent oracle: `image` crate docs for format magic bytes (independent of handler code); mastodon.social live Account fixture showing avatar/header URL structure; reference path: encode a known PNG via `image` crate outside the handler, compare dimensions.
- Test approach: integration test with real in-memory SQLite and temp filesystem dir; happy paths: JPEG upload, PNG upload, WebP upload; edge cases: file too large (>2MB) → 422, unsupported type (SVG magic bytes) → 422, HEIC → JPEG conversion.

## Defensive programming checklist
- Detect MIME from magic bytes, not Content-Type header (clients lie)
- Enforce 2MB max before reading full body to avoid memory exhaustion
- Atomic write: write to temp path in same dir, then rename (same filesystem)
- No `.unwrap()` in handler paths
- If `image` crate cannot decode HEIC (feature not compiled in), return 422 with clear message
- Do not store the original unprocessed file — only the resized output
